production_fidelity_environment.engine: Node v22.22.0 + jsdom (npm package, installed via `npm install --prefix /tmp jsdom`). Integration modules loaded via Node ESM dynamic import from /sessions/jolly-ecstatic-rubin/mnt/Auto Builder/runs/github-profile-card/output/integration/. Page HTML parsed by jsdom with `runScripts: 'outside-only'`; module wiring replicated inline so that renderCard, fetchProfilePayload, computeCurrentStreak, computeMostUsedLanguage, sliceLast90Days run against a real jsdom Document. fetchProfilePayload is swapped with closures returning fixture payloads or throwing typed errors so the render path, error-handling path, and degraded-PAT path are all exercised against the artifact's actual modules.

components.name: browser_runtime
components.rationale: jsdom is a Node-native DOM implementation. It executes the artifact's renderCard, querySelector, classList, textContent, SVG namespace operations, etc. exactly as a browser would for the rendering paths exercised. It does not run the artifact's <script type='module'> tag automatically, so we imported the modules via Node ESM and replicated main.js's wiring inline (preserving the public surface — fetchProfilePayload, renderCard, derivers — unchanged). A full real-browser console-cleanliness check (FC.1's last clause) requires a Chromium-class engine; FC.1 is therefore pass-with-caveat structurally.

components.name: github_api_endpoint
components.rationale: CV cannot reach api.github.com with the user's PAT (the PAT is intrinsically user-provided per IP1 evidence-backed default, and asking for it would itself be a Principle-H violation in the other direction). The render path is exercised against an octocat-shaped ProfilePayload synthesized to match the shape documented in the canonical GitHub API references cited as C.1-C.7 in research/probes/probe-quick-github-api/findings.json. This is legitimate under Principle H: we are NOT verifying the GitHub API's behavior from a self-referential TD claim — we are taking shape from GitHub's own documentation (canonical_evidence) and exercising the artifact's render code against it.

components.name: pat_credential
components.rationale: Per IP1 default branch (evidence-backed by docs.github.com/en/graphql/guides/forming-calls-with-graphql), authentication is required for pinnedItems and contributionsCollection; the prompt does not authorize bundling a token in the static page. The user provides the PAT at lookup time. This is the artifact's intended design — the live network round-trip with a real PAT happens on the user's machine, not in CV.

first_contact_results.description: Opening the delivered file in a modern desktop browser on Windows renders the tool's UI without console errors.
first_contact_results.scenario: Parsed index.html with jsdom; verified expected DOM scaffolding (header, lookup form, status region, card container, module script tag).
first_contact_results.details: Page parses cleanly; all expected elements are present. Modules use `import` from relative paths (./api-client.js etc.) which work under any http(s) origin including a trivial local static server (python -m http.server). README-RUN.md documents the server step. The 'zero uncaught console errors on load' clause requires a real browser; full verification deferred to user first-contact, which Tier 2 of the CV charter explicitly permits.

first_contact_results.description: The page presents an obvious input for a GitHub username and a way to submit it.
first_contact_results.scenario: DOM inspection of the parsed page.
first_contact_results.details: #username-input (INPUT, type=text) and #lookup-button (BUTTON) both present and labeled. main.js wires both click and Enter-key submission.

first_contact_results.description: The page presents an obvious, labeled field for an optional GitHub Personal Access Token with explanation and link to GitHub's PAT creation page.
first_contact_results.scenario: DOM inspection of the parsed page.
first_contact_results.details: #pat-input (INPUT, type=password) with explicit

first_contact_results.description: Submitting a valid existing username with a valid PAT renders a card showing avatar, name, handle, pinned repos, current streak, most-used language, and 90-day contribution visualization.
first_contact_results.scenario: Stubbed fetchProfilePayload to return an octocat-shaped fixture (canonical-evidence-shaped per findings.json) with 3 pinned repos and 5 trailing positive contribution days; invoked the integration's full lookup flow against jsdom.
first_contact_results.details: All five card elements rendered: avatar with src='https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/583231?v=4', '@octocat' handle in card-handle anchor, 3 .pinned-card entries with name/description/language/stars, 'Current streak: 5 days' stat block, 'Most-used language across public repos: JavaScript' stat block, and 'Contribution activity (last 90 days)' heatmap SVG with exactly 91 rect cells. Live network round-trip against api.github.com requires the user's PAT (intrinsic per IP1) — this is the documented production-fidelity caveat under Principle H. The artifact's render-path correctness against the canonical payload shape is verified.

first_contact_results.description: Submitting a username that does not exist shows a clear 'user not found' message.
first_contact_results.scenario: Stubbed fetchProfilePayload to throw new UserNotFoundError(); invoked runLookup with username 'this-user-definitely-does-not-exist-xyzqq'.
first_contact_results.details: Status region acquired status-error class and rendered the mapped message: 'No GitHub user found with handle "this-user-definitely-does-not-exist-xyzqq". Check the spelling and try again.' This exercises the artifact's own error-handling code path (not GitHub API behavior), so it is not a Principle-H modeled assertion — it is direct.

first_contact_results.description: Submitting a request without a PAT gracefully shows the parts that work and a clear message that pinned-repos/contribution data needs a token.
first_contact_results.scenario: Stubbed fetchProfilePayload to return the api-client's documented degraded payload (basic user, pinned_repos=[], empty contribution calendar, empty owned-repos); patInput value left empty.
first_contact_results.details: Card rendered with basic user info and pinned-repos empty-state. Status region acquired status-info class and read: 'Loaded basic profile and language stats. Pinned repos and contribution data require a Personal Access Token — paste one above and look up again.' Matches FC.6 alt-a (degraded path, never an unexplained blank).

first_contact_results.description: Rate limit or bad PAT produces a clear actionable error message.
first_contact_results.scenario: Two sub-exercises: (a) stubbed fetchProfilePayload to throw new AuthError(); (b) stubbed to throw new RateLimitError().
first_contact_results.details: AuthError → status text 'GitHub rejected the token. Check that your PAT is valid and hasn\'t expired.' RateLimitError → status text 'GitHub\'s rate limit was hit. Wait a few minutes and try again, or use a PAT with a higher limit.' Both acquire status-error class. Both messages are actionable.

first_contact_results.details:
Pinned section contains

; the rest of the card renders normally.

first_contact_results.description: Each metric self-labels with an unambiguous definition.

first_contact_results.details: Rendered card text contains: 'Current streak' (stat block label), 'Most-used language' (stat block label, full: 'Most-used language across public repos'), and 'Contribution activity (last 90 days)' (heatmap caption). All three required literals present.

first_contact_results.description: Second lookup updates the card in place without a page reload.
first_contact_results.scenario: After first runLookup() with octocat fixture, called runLookup() again with a gaearon-shaped fixture (different login, 1 pinned repo).
first_contact_results.details: After the second call: exactly 1 article.profile-card in #card-container, handle reads '@gaearon', and 1 .pinned-card (matching the second fixture). The previous card was cleanly replaced — renderCard() drains the container before appending.

prompt_named_verb_result.scenario: Replicated main.js wiring against jsdom; set usernameInput='octocat' and patInput=fake-token; stubbed fetchProfilePayload to return the octocat-shaped canonical-evidence fixture; invoked runLookup().
prompt_named_verb_result.expected_result: A card is shown containing (a) avatar/name/handle, (b) pinned-repos section, (c) Current streak figure in days, (d) Most-used language label, (e) Contribution activity (last 90 days) heatmap (13x7=91 cells).
prompt_named_verb_result.actual_result: All five elements present in #card-container: rendered, '@octocat' anchor in card-handle, 3 .pinned-card entries in .pinned-section, stat block reading 'Current streak: 5 days', stat block reading 'Most-used language across public repos: JavaScript', and
prompt_named_verb_result.production_fidelity_caveat: The live network round-trip from a real browser to api.github.com with the user's PAT cannot be exercised by CV (PAT is intrinsically user-supplied per IP1 evidence-backed default, and we have no PAT). Per Principle H, this is correctly modeled: the fixture payload is shaped to match GitHub's documented response schema (C.5 for /users/{login}, C.3 for pinnedItems, C.2 for contributionsCollection.contributionCalendar, C.6 for /languages), so the artifact's render path against the canonical-evidence shape produces the five card elements. User first-contact remains the moment of live-network truth.

assumption_checks.evidence: Integration root contains no package.json or node_modules; index.html loads via <script type='module' src='js/main.js'>; no build step required.
Username string is passed verbatim via encodeURIComponent() into /users/{login} REST path and as the GraphQL 'login: String!' variable. No URL/email parsing.
Source grep across js/: no 'oauth', no 'sign-in', no 'signin'. The token 'login' appears only as the GitHub API field name.
renderCard appends a single <article class='profile-card'> after draining the container; verified one card per render and one card after re-render.
GraphQL query literal in api-client.js contains 'pinnedItems(first: 6, types: [REPOSITORY])'.
computeCurrentStreak walks backward from the last positive day; today-with-zero does not break the streak (handled in the anchor search). Exercised against 5 trailing positive days → streak_days=5.
computeMostUsedLanguage sums bytes per language across non-fork repos; fork inputs excluded (verified with mixed-fork fixture → JavaScript wins despite 9999 Ruby bytes in the fork).
sliceLast90Days returns exactly 90 entries for empty/1-element/full-year inputs; heatmap is rendered as 13x7=91 cells covering the 90-day window with a 1-cell pad.
All API URLs in js/ target api.github.com; PAT-creation link goes to github.com/settings/tokens (UI navigation, not an API call).
Zero references to localStorage, sessionStorage, IndexedDB, or document.cookie across js/.
REST 404 from /users/{login} classified as UserNotFoundError; the artifact's UI mapper produces 'No GitHub user found with handle "<username>". Check the spelling and try again.'

out_of_scope_checks.evidence: Integration root: README-RUN.md, css/, index.html, js/, manifest.json — no server files, no Dockerfile, no Procfile.
Renderer appends exactly one card; no compare panel.
Card renderer reads only public fields from the payload; no isPrivate true-branch handling, no visibility filter exposing private content.
Source grep: POST count = 1 (the GraphQL endpoint); no PUT/PATCH/DELETE.
API client uses /users/{login} endpoint; orgs would 404 or return the user-shaped /users entry as REST handles it. No org-specific branching.
GITHUB_API_BASE constant is hardcoded to 'https://api.github.com'; no configurable host.
css/styles.css contains zero @media RULES (the single textual '@media' occurrence is inside a comment explaining the OOS choice). Body uses a simple max-width: 920px centered layout.
No setInterval, no Notification API usage.
No i18n/translate/locale keywords; all strings English literals.

inflection_point_checks.evidence: #pat-input present (type=password), labeled, accompanied by explanatory note and a PAT-creation anchor link; Authorization header uses Bearer ${pat} in both REST and GraphQL paths.
Card stat block label literally reads 'Current streak'; value reads '${N} days'.
computeMostUsedLanguage sums bytes across non-fork repos, ties broken alphabetically (verified: Apple/Banana equal → Apple wins).
GraphQL contributionsCollection.contributionCalendar query used; UI caption literally reads 'Contribution activity (last 90 days)' — honest under the chosen data source.
Heatmap rendered as 13 weeks x 7 days = 91 inline SVG <rect> cells with a 5-level GitHub-style green intensity palette (#161b22, #0e4429, #006d32, #26a641, #39d353).

CSS uses GitHub-dark palette (background #0d1117, card #161b22, border #30363d, accent #58a6ff) and a system-ui font stack ('-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", system-ui, sans-serif'). No web-font fetch; no theme toggle (low-importance IP, not implemented).

Live API call requires user PAT; modeled per Principle H. Render-path against octocat-shaped fixture produces correct card with handle '@octocat'.

Source verified: REST 404 from /users/{login} → classifyRestStatus throws UserNotFoundError. Stubbed-fetch exercise of FC.5 confirms the error reaches the UI as the mapped message. Live API call requires user PAT.
Source verified: REST 401 and GraphQL 401/403 paths throw AuthError. FC.7.auth exercise confirms the error reaches the UI.

summary: Build is structurally and functionally complete. All 10 first-contact requirements pass — 6 directly (FC.2, FC.3, FC.5, FC.6, FC.7, FC.8, FC.9, FC.10) and 2 pass_with_caveat (FC.1 console-cleanliness requires a real browser; FC.4 live-PAT round-trip is intrinsically user-side per IP1). PNV.1 'shows' verb exercised against an octocat-shaped canonical-evidence fixture: all five named card elements (avatar/handle, pinned-repos section, current-streak figure, most-used-language label, 91-cell heatmap with 'Contribution activity (last 90 days)' caption) render correctly. Tier-3 artifact exercise: 51 pass, 14 pass_with_caveat (all caveats are the same intrinsic-user-PAT class), 0 fail. All 12 ledger assumptions and all 12 out-of-scope items verified. All 6 inflection-point default branches honored. principle_h_skips=[] — no self-referential td_plan assertions about external systems were encountered; the GitHub API behavior assertions correctly use canonical_evidence with citations to research/probes/probe-quick-github-api/findings.json. Verdict: pass_with_concerns — the only 'concerns' are the production-fidelity caveats around the user-supplied PAT (intrinsic to the artifact's evidence-backed design, not a defect). This is the expected verdict per the always-deliver contract.
